summary: Good audience recording captures an intimate night with a standout Comes A Time solo, though the show was marred by a bottle incident.
review: The audience‑sourced tape does a solid job of conveying the small‑venue intimacy and the beauty of Jerry Garcia’s solo on Comes A Time. Reviewers highlight the jam and space sections as high points, but one reviewer notes a disruptive bottle throw that hit Bill Kreutzmann’s kit during the second set.

ai_rating.rationale: Two moderately positive reviews (4 and 3 stars) praise the performance and recording, while a single zero‑star review focuses on an incident rather than audio quality; overall rating falls in the average range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Captures the sound of the night well with clear ambience; no major drop‑outs or muddiness reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Intimate East Coast arena gave a tighter feel; second set was interrupted by a bottle thrown at Bill's drums during Comes A Time, leading to a brief on‑stage tension.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Solo on Comes A Time described as beautiful and ripping.
band_member_comments.Bob: Mentioned breaking equipment after the incident, canceling an encore.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill was hit by a bottle during Comes A Time and responded with a finger gesture to the crowd.
